prompt:
You are Agent 1 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-PlNDkg.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Design and implement the foundation of a notification system.

Specifically:
1. Choose an architecture for notifications: event-driven (EventBus with subscribers) OR direct service calls. Commit to ONE approach.
2. Define the notification service interface and core types (NotificationType, NotificationPayload, etc.) in src/services/ and src/models/.
3. Implement the base notification service with the chosen architecture.
4. Create an email notification adapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (stub is fine, but interface must be real).
5. Document your architectural decision — the pattern you chose, why, and what constraints it imposes.
6. Make sure the codebase compiles.

Important:
- Pick ONE pattern (event-driven OR direct calls) and commit to it. Do not mix approaches.
- Define clear interfaces that other developers will implement against.
- Document constraints: e.g., "All adapters must implement NotificationAdapter interface" or "All notifications must go through EventBus.emit()".
- 11 more developers will build on your foundation.

parameters.task: Design and implement notification system foundation: choose event-driven vs direct calls architecture, define core types/interfaces, implement base notification service, create email adapter

parameters.summary: Notification system uses event-driven architecture (EventBus) exclusively, not direct calls
parameters.context: The codebase has two patterns: EventBus (used for order:created) and CallbackRegistry (used for order:status-changed). The notification service comment explicitly flags this inconsistency. The package.json description says "event-driven notifications". The EventBus already supports typed events, async handlers, event logging, and unsubscribe. 11 more developers will build on this foundation.
parameters.rationale: EventBus is the right choice: (1) already established as the primary pattern in index.ts docs and order.service.ts, (2) supports async handlers needed for real notification delivery, (3) has event log for debugging/replay, (4) typed events provide compile-time safety, (5) loose coupling means new notification channels can be added without modifying emitters. The CallbackRegistry usage for status-changed is the anomaly to fix.

alternatives.option: Direct service calls via CallbackRegistry pattern
alternatives.reason_rejected: Tighter coupling, no event log, synchronous-only, inconsistent with existing EventBus usage and documented architecture
cons: Tighter coupling between producer and consumer

alternatives.reason_rejected: Explicitly flagged as a problem in notification.service.ts comments. Two patterns for the same concern creates confusion for 11 downstream developers
cons: Ambiguous which pattern to use for new features

constraints: All notifications must flow through EventBus.emit() - no direct service calls for notification triggers
All delivery channels must implement the NotificationAdapter interface
NotificationService subscribes to events and dispatches to adapters - it is the only consumer of notification-related events
assumptions: The EventBus singleton pattern is acceptable for this application scale
Async notification delivery is acceptable (no synchronous delivery guarantee needed)
In-memory event log is sufficient (no persistent event store needed yet)

parameters.old_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * Architecture note: Order events are used to trigger side effects
 * (notifications, analytics, etc.) without direct coupling.
 * The OrderService emits events; it does not directly call
 * the NotificationService.
 */
parameters.new_string:
/**
 * Order service — business logic for order management.
 *
 * Depends on:
 * - OrderRepository (data layer) for persistence
 * - UserRepository (data layer) for user validation
 * - EventBus (event system) for publishing order lifecycle events
 *
 * All order lifecycle events (creation, status changes) are emitted
 * via EventBus. NotificationService and other listeners subscribe
 * independently — no direct coupling.
 */

parameters.summary: All notifications must flow through EventBus — no direct service calls or CallbackRegistry
parameters.detail: The notification system uses event-driven architecture exclusively. Services emit domain events via EventBus.emit(). NotificationService subscribes to those events and dispatches to registered NotificationAdapters. The CallbackRegistry pattern was removed from the notification flow. Order status changes now go through EventBus like all other events.

parameters.summary: All notification delivery channels must implement the NotificationAdapter interface
parameters.detail: New delivery channels (SMS, webhook, in-app, etc.) must implement NotificationAdapter from src/models/notification.ts. The interface requires a `channel` property and an async `send(payload)` method. Register adapters with NotificationService.registerAdapter(). See EmailAdapter in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts as the reference implementation.

parameters.summary: CallbackRegistry in src/utils/callback-registry.ts is no longer used by notification system
parameters.detail: The statusChangeCallbacks export in callback-registry.ts is no longer imported by order.service.ts or notification.service.ts. The CallbackRegistry class itself remains for potential non-notification uses, but the notification system exclusively uses EventBus now. If nothing else uses statusChangeCallbacks, it can be removed.

parameters.summary: Agent 1 complete: Notification system foundation implemented with event-driven architecture
parameters.detail:
Completed:
1. ARCHITECTURE DECISION: Event-driven (EventBus) exclusively — no direct calls. Recorded in Twining.
2. CORE TYPES defined in src/models/notification.ts: NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationStatus, NotificationPayload, Notification, NotificationResult, NotificationAdapter interface.
3. NotificationService rewritten in src/services/notification.service.ts: subscribes to EventBus events, dispatches to registered adapters, tracks notification lifecycle.
4. EmailAdapter created in src/adapters/email.adapter.ts implementing NotificationAdapter.
5. OrderService fixed: status changes now emit via EventBus (removed CallbackRegistry usage).
6. All tests updated and passing (70/70). TypeScript compiles clean.

Key files created/modified:
- NEW: src/models/notification.ts (types + NotificationAdapter interface)
- NEW: src/adapters/email.adapter.ts (email delivery adapter)
- MODIFIED: src/services/notification.service.ts (rewritten for EventBus + adapter pattern)
- MODIFIED: src/services/order.service.ts (status changes via EventBus)
- MODIFIED: src/index.ts (exports new types + adapter)
- MODIFIED: tests/services/notification.service.test.ts (updated for new architecture)
- MODIFIED: tests/services/order.service.test.ts (updated event count assertion)

parameters.command:
git add src/models/notification.ts src/adapters/email.adapter.ts src/services/notification.service.ts src/services/order.service.ts src/index.ts tests/services/notification.service.test.ts tests/services/order.service.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Implement event-driven notification system foundation

Unify on EventBus architecture (removing CallbackRegistry from notification
flow). Define NotificationAdapter interface for delivery channels, add core
notification types, rewrite NotificationService to subscribe to events and
dispatch to adapters, and create EmailAdapter as reference implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ec5eb7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+# Project Guidelines
+
+## Architecture
+- This project follows the repository pattern for data access
+- Services depend on repositories, never on each other directly
+- Events are preferred over direct cross-service calls for decoupling
+- All business logic lives in the service layer
+
+## Coding Conventions
+- Use TypeScript strict mode — no `any` types
+- All public methods must have JSDoc comments
+- Use dependency injection via constructor parameters
+- Error handling: throw typed errors, never return null for errors
+- Prefer async/await over raw Promises
+
+## Testing
+- Tests use vitest
+- Each module has a corresponding test file in tests/
+- Mock external dependencies, test business logic directly
+- Minimum: test the happy path and one error path per public method
+
+## File Organization
+- src/models/ — Data models and interfaces
+- src/repositories/ — Data access layer (implements repository interfaces)
+- src/services/ — Business logic layer
+- src/events/ — Event definitions and event bus
+- src/utils/ — Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
+- src/config/ — Configuration files
+- tests/ — Test files mirroring src/ structure
+
+## Git Practices
+- Commit atomically per logical change
+- Write descriptive commit messages explaining the "why"
+- Run tests before committing
